title: Average rent now swallows 98.7% of young workers’ pay for one-person flats in Spain
summary: The youth emancipation rate fell to 14.5% in 2025, the worst figure on record, says Spain's Youth Council, adding that a young person must spend almost all their wages to rent a home alone.
justification: Major social crisis (98.7% rent burden) directly connects to price elasticity of supply, income distribution, and labor market dynamics, with the extreme figure being genuinely shocking.

title: German business morale improves despite Iran war disruption to energy markets
summary: German business confidence rose unexpectedly in May, breaking a run of declines linked to the Iran war and offering cautious optimism that Europe's largest economy is proving more resilient than initially feared.
justification: Counterintuitive improvement in business confidence despite war disruption connects to macroeconomic indicators and consumer/business sentiment, with the resilience being genuinely surprising.

title: Million-dollar salaries, billion-dollar stakes: Top AI talent every tech giant is fighting over
summary: As Big Tech and a whole cohort of new-generation AI start-ups race towards artificial general intelligence (AGI), elite researchers and engineering leaders have become the equivalent of franchise athletes.
justification: Significant tech talent competition connects to HRM and competitive advantage, with the extreme salary escalation and comparison to professional sports franchising being genuinely unusual.

title: Nvidia beats expectations again as AI boom drives revenue surge, but shares slip
summary: The world's most valuable company beat forecasts as revenues jumped 85%. Yet rising competition and efforts to diversify beyond big tech left investors cautious, sending shares lower in late trading.
justification: Market leader beating expectations but declining is counterintuitive and connects to market structure, competition, and investor sentiment in a genuinely surprising way.

title: SpaceX IPO ready for launch as countdown begins for what could be the biggest ever listing
summary: SpaceX plans a record-breaking IPO that could raise around $75 billion (€64.5bn) despite ongoing losses. The Elon Musk-led firm says funds will support Moon and Mars missions, while strong Starlink earnings contrast with losses in X and xAI.
justification: Record IPO despite ongoing losses is paradoxical and connects to corporate finance, IPOs, and investor risk appetite in a genuinely unusual way.

title: European companies are reindustrialising — just with less money
summary: Planned investment for the next three years is falling even as AI cements its role as a crucial economic driver.
justification: Paradox of reindustrialization with falling investment is counterintuitive and connects to capital investment and productivity, presenting a genuinely unusual tension.

title: Edmund Phelps, Who Upended the Way We View Inflation, Dies at 92
summary: He won a Nobel in 2006 for challenging the conventional wisdom among economists that higher inflation was a necessary price to pay for low unemployment.
justification: Obituary of Nobel laureate who fundamentally challenged inflation-unemployment theory (Phillips Curve), highly curriculum-relevant to IB Economics macro, and interesting as a reminder of how economic thinking evolves.

title: Kushner’s Thrive Capital Makes a $100M Investment in Shopify Inc. (SHOP)
justification: Significant venture capital investment by political figure in major e-commerce company, relevant to IB Business (finance, investment), unusual due to political connection and size.

title: Law firm Fenwick agrees to pay $54 million in settlement over FTX work
justification: Major law firm settlement over FTX work significant for corporate accountability and ethics, highly relevant to IB Business (corporate governance, professional responsibility), and unusual as a major professional firm paying for client failure consequences.

title: Shein to buy apparel retailer Everlane
justification: Shein acquiring legacy retailer Everlane is a major strategic move in fast fashion, linking to market consolidation and competitive strategy, and genuinely surprising given Shein's low-cost dominance acquiring a premium sustainability-focused brand.

title: Analysis:Space ETFs booming in anticipation of SpaceX IPO
justification: Investor speculation on SpaceX IPO through space-sector ETFs illustrates market expectations and speculative behavior, and unusually demonstrates how anticipated corporate actions drive financial instrument demand before official announcements.

title: Analysis:ECB, banks rift hampers Europe's efforts to loosen reliance on US payments giants
justification: European efforts to reduce US payment system dependence is significant geopolitical economic strategy, linking to trade and financial system structures, and notably shows institutional tension between regulators and private banks on critical infrastructure.

title: European Commission proposes Russia sanctions reprieve for dealings with Chinese chips firm
justification: EU sanctions policy adjustment for Chinese semiconductor dealings is significant geopolitical trade strategy, and surprisingly shows economic pragmatism overriding security concerns in response to technology competition.

title: Nvidia can deliver chips — but it can’t buy Big Tech out of its credit and power-grid crisis
summary: Corporate profits can’t fix a chaotic trade war with China, climbing credit premiums and AI infrastructure limits.
justification: Analysis of Nvidia's limits despite chip supply links to infrastructure constraints and macro challenges facing tech giants, and usefully presents the counterintuitive view that supply-side dominance cannot solve systemic credit and trade problems.

title: Your bond portfolio is facing a ‘termite’ infestation far worse than Jamie Dimon’s ‘cockroaches’
summary: JP Morgan Chase CEO warned of credit-market ‘cockroaches.’ But opaque AI loans and excessive leverage — ‘credit termites’ — are hollowing out the economy.

title: These 4 market sectors look frothy — and Nvidia’s isn’t even the biggest bubble

justification: Market valuation analysis identifying multiple overheated sectors links to asset pricing and market structure, and presents the counterintuitive framing that a 30% crash risk is 'good news' compared to broader concerns.

title: ‘Buffett Indicator’ warns of a market top — 8 crucial signs that stocks are running on fumes
summary: From a grotesque 230% valuation gap to a ticking energy crisis, this unstoppable market is more fragile than it looks.

title: Morocco farmers saw hope in rain, but Mideast war inflates production costs
summary: After years of drought, Moroccan farmers looked forward to an abundant harvest this year. Heavy rains over the winter saw parched landscapes turn green and farmers went back to their fields. But their hopes were quickly shattered when fuel and fertiliser costs skyrocketed due to the war in Iran.
justification: Major agricultural story with strong links to elasticity, supply chains, and macro shocks, genuinely counterintuitive that weather improvement is negated by geopolitical cost inflation.

justification: Wage deal controversy revealing internal inequality between divisions is significant and unusual, linking to HRM, organizational structure, and fairness.

justification: Major e-commerce firm's aggressive pricing strategy threatening small suppliers is significant and counterintuitive, linking to market power, predatory pricing, and stakeholder impact.

Samsung Electronics started trading at 300,000 won in regular trading on the day, rising to as high as 300,500 won.

justification: Major JV dissolution with strategic shift at major battery manufacturer is significant and links to organizational structure/strategy, and counterintuitive that SK On gains full control after Ford exit.

justification: Federal Reserve chair appointment is major macroeconomic development with clear curriculum relevance, and Trump's attempt to influence central bank independence is genuinely unusual and counterintuitive.
